Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Erica Chang; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

3 - DEFENDANT PHUNG TRAN, TAM NGUYEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One.

Defendants Tam Nguyen and Phung Kim Tam's motion to compel Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran's responses to their Form Interrogatories is granted. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Plaintiffs have not established that they have provided any responses to the Form Interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Form Interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290.)

Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses to Defendants' Form Interrogatories by October 24, 2025.

Plaintiffs have not demonstrated they acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall pay $560 to Moving Defendants as sanctions, payment no later than October 24, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290(c).)

To the extent Defendants seek additional or different relief, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Erica Chang; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANTs PHUNG TRAN, TAM NGUYEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Defendants Tam Nguyen and Phung Kim Tam's motion to compel Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran's responses to her Request For Production Of Documents ("RFPs") is granted. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) The record demonstrates Plaintiffs did not provide any responses to the RFPs. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for requests for production of documents when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300.)

Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall serve verified, objection-free, responses to Defendants' RFPs, Set One by October 15, 2025.

Plaintiffs have not demonstrated they acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall pay $560 to Moving Defendants as sanctions, payment no later than October 24, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(c).)

To the extent Defendants seek additional or different relief, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

2 - DEFENDANTs PHUNG TRAN, TAM NGUYEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One.

Defendants Tam Nguyen and Phung Kim Tam's motion to compel Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran's responses to her Special Interrogatories is granted. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Plaintiffs have not established that he has provided any responses to the Special Interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Special Interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290.)

Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses to Defendants' Special Interrogatories by October 24, 2025.

Plaintiffs have not demonstrated they acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiffs Maria Rodriguez Cruz, Suzannah Beltran and Edgar Beltran shall pay $560 to Moving Defendants as sanctions, payment no later than October 24, 2025.
